revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Inatsuhiko-jinja]][[ja:為那都比古神社]] {{Expand Japanese|為那都比古神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Inatsuhiko Shrine | native_name = 為那都比古神社 | image = Inatsuhiko-jinja, haiden-2.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Inatsuhiko Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.839543|135.497484}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Inatsuhiko Shrine'''|為那都比古神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Commons category (P373) == * Inatsuhiko-jinja == has part(s) (P527) == * {{ill|Engishiki seat|lt=Engishiki seat|qid=Q135018062|1=Engishiki seat}} ** P1114: {'amount': '+2', 'unit': '1'} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府箕面市石丸2-10-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Inatsuhiko-jinja}} [[JA:為那都比古神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11567184}} == Japanese content == {{Otheruses|Shrines in Minoo City, Osaka Prefecture|Shrines in Ikeda City, Osaka Prefecture|Inatsuhiko Shrine}} {{Shrine |Name = Tamenatsuhiko Shrine |Image = [[File:Inatsuhiko-jinja, haiden-2.jpg|250px]]
House of worship |Location = {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minoh City|Q665097|qid=Q665097|1=Minoh}}{{ill|lt=Ishimaru (Minoh City)|UNKNOWN|lt=Ishimaru}}2-10-1 |Position = {{Wiki coordinates 2 steps degrees minutes seconds|34|50|23.00|N|135|29|50.81|E|region:JP-27_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Tamenatuhiko Shrine}} |Enshrined deity = Tamenatsuhiko Okami
Tamenatsuhime Okami |Divinity = |Shakaku = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (2nd grade)
Old {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Foundation = unknown |Main shrine = |alias= |Fudasho, etc. = |Regular Festival = {{ill|lt=April 15th|Q2497|qid=Q2497|1=April 15}} (Spring Festival)
{{ill|lt=October 14th|Q2922|qid=Q2922|1=October 14}} (Autumn Festival) |Shinto ritual = |Map = {{Location map+|Japan Osaka|width=200|float=center|caption=|places={{Location map~|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=46|lat_sec=5.84|lon_deg=135|lon_min=25|lon_sec=34.17|position=bottom|label_size=85|label=Kawabe-gun
Tamenago|mark=red_pog.svg|marksize=10}} {{Location map~|Japan Osaka|lat_deg=34|lat_min=50|lat_sec=23.00|lon_deg=135|lon_min=29|lon_sec=50.81|position=top|label_size=90|label=Shinto torii
Shrine|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=22}}}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} [[File:Inatsuhiko-jinja, torii-2.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Torii}}]]'''Tamenatsuhiko Shrine'' (Inatsuhiko Jinja) is located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Minoh City|Q665097|qid=Q665097|1=Minoh}}Ishimaru. In {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=Old company rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} is {{ill|lt=Murasha|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined God === The following eight deities are enshrined. . ; Main enshrined deity :* Tamenatsuhiko Okami :* Tamenatsuhime Okami ; jointly enshrined deity :* {{ill|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|Q455602|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Omikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Oyamakui no Kami|Q10935105|qid=Q10935105|1=Oyamakui no Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Konohana no Sakuyabime|Q1781862|qid=Q1781862|lt=Konohanasakuyahimekami|1=Konohanasakuya-hime}} :: {{ill|lt=Amagiya Mikoto|Q2564508|qid=Q2564508|lt=Amagoyane Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane}}, {{ill|lt=Kagutsuchi|Q1640658|qid=Q1640658|lt=Hinokagu Earth God|1=Kagu-tsuchi}}, {{ill|lt=Michizane Sugawara|Q382005|qid=Q382005|1=Sugawara no Michizane}} {{ill|lt=extension (Era name)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}} "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" established in 5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=In the Shinto Name Book|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}, there is a mention of ``Tamenatuhiko Shrine 2,'' and these 2 shrines can be compared to the two gods Tamenatuhiko-kami and Tamenatuhime-kami {{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. However, Tamenatsuhime is a deity enshrined at the former Omiya Shrine (currently closed; the site is Hakushima, Minoh City), and was enshrined in our company in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}40 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}}).{{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. Other shrines including Amaterasu Omikami were also enshrined together in 1901. === About the enshrined deity === [[File:Daigu-ji (Mino), hondou.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Iouzan Daiguji (Hakushima, Minoh City)}}]] The ``Ina'' in the names of the two main deities (Tamenatsuhikokami and Tamenatsuhimekami) are derived from place names. In addition to "Tamena", it is also written as "Ina", "Tamena", "Tamena", etc. {{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Gods)|2000}}, {{ill|lt=Inagawa|Q11571867|qid=Q11571867|1=Ina River}} basin ({{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Toshima District (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11633558|qid=Q11633558|lt=Toshima County|1=Teshima district}}/{{ill|lt=Kawabe County|Q1042053|qid=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe County|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}}) It was the name of a wide area. Place names like this There are many known examples of gods and people with the name Hiko/Hime, such as {{ill|lt=Kibitsuhiko no Mikoto|Q65318|qid=Q65318|lt=Kibitsuhiko|1=Kibitsuhiko-no-mikoto}} and {{ill|lt=Isetsuhiko|Q17193127|qid=Q17193127|1=Isetsuhiko}}, and the case of ``Tamenatsuhiko/Hime'' is the same. It is assumed that this was the deification of the chieftains of powerful clans in the Ina region. However, there are various opinions as to which clan it is specifically compared to (see {{ill|lt=#Research|UNKNOWN|lt=Review section}}).Also, as mentioned above, Tamenatsuhime is the enshrined deity of Omiya Shrine in Hakushima, Minoh City {{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. The two gods of Tamenatsuhiko and Tamenatsuhime lived for {{ill|lt=Kanpei|Q1346292|qid=Q1346292|1=Kanpyō (era)}} years ({{ill|lt=889 years|Q23833|qid=Q23833|1=889}}-{{ja link|898|Q24131}}), it is said to have been divided into separate shrines. is written in the Katsuo-ji Temple document of {{ill|lt=Masayoshi|Q1327489|qid=Q1327489|1=Shōka (era)}} 3rd year ({{ill|lt=1259|Q5507|qid=Q5507|1=1259}}), which states, The description of ``Tenma Tenjin'' is recognized. This Omiya Shrine was abolished after being enshrined in 1901, and only the Yakushi-do Hall remains of the former {{ill|lt=Jinguji Temple|Q1074971|qid=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} {{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. Near the site, there is a huge rock (Yoga Rock/Io Rock) that is said to have been visited by a god, and in addition, in "{{ill|lt=Setyo Gundan|Q11498591|qid=Q11498591|1=Setyo Gundan}}" and "Settsu Shi" Since only the explanation of Omiya Shrine is included in the section on Tamenatsuhiko Shrine, there is also a theory that Omiya Shrine is the main shrine of the Shikinai shrine ``Tamenatuhiko Shrine Niza'' {{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Gods)|2000}}.Furthermore, at one time, the deity enshrined at Tamenatsuhiko Shrine was {{ill|lt=Gozu Tenno|Q11570247|qid=Q11570247|1=Gozu Tennō}}, and the shrine itself was also called ``Tenno'' {{Sfn|Tamenatuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Regarding this, in "{{ill|lt=Settsu Famous Places Picture Book|Q11498549|qid=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}", it is said that in the {{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} years ({{ill|lt=1573|Q6678|qid=Q6678|1=1573}}-{{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|qid=Q6769|1=1592}}), When Nobunaga|Q171411}} had {{ill|lt=Ukon Takayama|Q749873|qid=Q749873|1=Justo Takayama}} rename the temples and shrines of Settsu Province, our Shinjin complained that the name of the god was changed to Gozu Tenno (Nobunaga's guardian deity), so he was spared the trouble.{{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. {{ill|lt=Inatsuhiko Shrine|Q119929655|qid=Q119929655|1=Inatsuhiko Shrine}} (Ubocho, Ikeda City) is also known as another shrine that enshrines Inatsuhiko. k|Round tomb|Q11394747}}There is a theory that the shrine that is enshrined on top of the shrine is the original Shikinai shrine "Tamenatsuhiko Shrine", but the details are not clear{{Sfn|Minoh City History Main volume Volume 1|1964|pp=41-53}}{{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. === History === Founding date is unknown. As mentioned above, it is thought that the shrine began with a ritual by a powerful clan living in the Ina region. It is known that a {{ill|lt=Dotaku|Q2312868|qid=Q2312868|1=Dōtaku}} (Nyoidani Dotaku) was excavated in Nyoidani, Minoh City, to the west of the precincts, and there is a theory that suggests that it is related to the Fuchigen of Tamenatsuhiko Shrine {{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. {{ill|lt=extension (era name)|Q1327274|qid=Q1327274|lt=Extended|1=Enchō}}5 years ({{ill|lt=927|Q30470|qid=Q30470|1=927}}) "{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}" {{ill|lt=Engishiki Shinmyocho|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|lt=Shinmyocho|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}} is {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}}{{ill|lt=Toshima County (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11633558|qid=Q11633558|lt=Toshima District|1=Teshima district}} is described as ``Tamenatuhiko Shrine 2nd Shrine'', and the 2nd shrine is listed in {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikiuchi|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286|lt=Shikinaisha}} {{Sfn|Tamenatuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}. Among the place names that appear in ``{{ill|lt=Japanese Ruijusho|Q2702542|qid=Q2702542|lt=Japanese Namesho|1=Wamyō Ruijushō}}'' from the middle of the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}}, this place is compared to Toshima District Ekiyago {{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Gods)|2000}}. The {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} festival is described in ``{{ill|lt=Settsu Meisho Zue|Q11498549|qid=Q11498549|1=Settsu Meisho Zue}}'' and other documents {{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.After {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, in {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}}5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|qid=Q7753|1=1872}}), {{ill|lt=Modern company rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} {jalink|Murasha|Q134917284}}, and was designated as a Shinto food shrine in February of 1907 ({{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}}). On June 14, 1907, in addition to the aforementioned Omiya Shrine, ten other shrines from the former {{ill|lt=Kayano Village (Osaka Prefecture)|Q11620526|qid=Q11620526|lt=Kayano Village|1=Kayano}} were enshrined together. === Precincts === File:Inatsuhiko-jinja, honden.jpg|Main shrine roof File:Inatsuhiko-jinja, Eirei-den.jpg|Heroic Souls Hall File:Inatsuhiko-jinja, shinmon-2.jpg|Shinmon === Setsusha === [[File:Inatsuhiko-jinja, Tenman-gu.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Tenmangu}}]] * Tenmangu Shrine === Historical evidence === [[File:Inadera-haiji1.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|{{ill|lt=Inadera Abandoned Temple Ruins|Q11571866|qid=Q11571866|1=Inadera haiji ato}}({{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Amagasaki City|Q213318|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}})}}{{small|While the Ina-dera Temple area is considered to be the center of the Ina region, Tamenatsuhiko Shrine is located at the eastern end of the Ina region. }}]] There are various theories regarding the Hosai clan of Tamenatsuhiko Shrine. As a factor for considering specific Hosai clans, it is known that in ``{{ill|lt=Newly Selected Shojiroku|Q1045362|qid=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'' the following clans whose headquarters are in Settsu Province are described. :* Settsu Province Kobetsu Tamena Masato - After the {{ill|lt=Emperor Senka|Q329728|qid=Q329728|1=Emperor Senka}} prince's Flame King. :* Lord Kawahara of Settsu Province - Same ancestor of Masato Tamena. After the Prince of Fire. During the reign of Emperor Tenji, he was given the surname of Kawahara. :* Sakyojinbetsu Inabezo - After {{ill|lt=Ikairoo|Q55536799|qid=Q55536799|lt=Ikagairoo no Mikoto|1=Ikagashiko-o}}. :* Settsu Province Morobaku Tamenabushi - Originated from Nakatsunabite from Baekje Province. :* Undetermined miscellaneous name in Settsu Province Tamenabushi - After Kinren, the 6th grandson of Ikagashiki no Mikoto.Among the above, Masato Tamena (Tamena clan) mentioned at the beginning is a clan that can be seen in {{ill|lt=Rokukokushi|Q1151168|qid=Q1151168|lt=Kokushi|1=Rikkokushi}}, and {{ill|lt=Nobutomo|Q4346747|qid=Q4346747|1=Ban Nobutomo}} is a clan mentioned in our company's festival in "{{ill|lt=Shinmeicho Kosho Doshiro|Q11588823|qid=Q11588823|1=Jinmyōchō Kōshō Dodai}}". There is a theory that the deity is compared to the ancestral deity of the Tamanatsuhiko people, and in ``{{ill|lt=Specially Selected Names of Gods|Q11571092|qid=Q11571092|1=Tokusen Jinmyōchō}}'' there is also a theory that the enshrined deity is compared to Prince Kamishiha, the son of Emperor Senka (the ancestor of the Prince Eba, Prince Iina/Kimi Iina).{{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Heibonsha)|1986}}.On the other hand, the ancient center of the Ina region (and {{ill|lt=Honkan|Q846706|qid=Q846706|lt=Honkanchi|1=Korean clans}} of the Tamena and Kawahara clans) was Kawabe District (Kawabe District) Tamenago (present-day {{ill|lt=Hyogo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ama) The northeastern part of Saki City|Q213318|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}} is believed to be {{Sfn|Tamena clan (Dictionary of Ancient Clan Names)|2010}}, and Tamenatuhiko Shrine is named after "Ina". Although it is a shrine, it is located at the eastern end of the Ina region (Ekiiego, Toshima District), so the theory that the Hosai clan is the Tamena clan is strongly denied {{Sfn|Minoh City History Main volume Volume 1 | 1964 | pp=41-53}}{{Sfn|Tenatsuhiko Shrine Niza (Shikinai Shrine) | 1977}}{{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Gods) | 2000}}.為奈氏以外の説としては、上記の{{ill|lt=物部氏|Q1755836|qid=Q1755836|1=Mononobe clan}}系・渡来系の猪名部氏（為奈部氏）と想定する説や、『摂陽群談』で大宮神社の祭祀にSince the Tokihara clan ({{ill|lt=Hata clan|Q987290|qid=Q987290|1=Hata clan}} branch clan) is involved, there is a theory that it is assumed to be the Hata clan {{Sfn|Tamenatsuhiko Shrine (Gods)|2000}}.In addition, "{{ill|lt=Nihon Shoki|Q813935|qid=Q813935|1=Nihon Shoki}}" states that "{{ill|lt=local government system of ancient Japan|Q11410912|qid=Q11410912|lt=Agatha|1=local government in classical Japan}}" jalink|Ina Prefecture|Q11571881}}", there is also a theory that the Hosai clan of our company served as the head of Ina Prefecture.{{Sfn|History of Minoh City Main volume Volume 1 | 1964 | pp=41-53}}{{Sfn|Ninatsuhiko Shrine Niza (Shikiuchi Shrine) | 1977}}. === Footnotes
